text:
We introduce Llama 3, a new family of foundation models supporting multilinguality, coding, reasoning and tool use on all scales. The core model is a dense Transformer with 405B parameters and a 128K-token context window, trained on a dataset of 15 trillion tokens. We validate the performance of our Llama 3.1 405B model through extensive experimentation, showing that its performance on our suite of benchmarks is on par with current closed-source models like GPT-4.

The main focus of our paper is on three of these levers and describes our data curation, scale computing and software architecture strategies. To match our models to human preferences and improve specific skills, we used an iterative post-training regimen of rejection sampling, supervised fine-tuning and direct preference optimization. We also describe a compositional approach to multimodality that seamlessly integrates image, video and speech recognition into the Llama 3 infrastructure. Multimodal variants of the models are currently under development, but the 8B, 70B and 405B models as well as the new safety classifiers — Llama Guard 3 — will be publicly available in order to encourage an open innovation process and help ensure that the AGI is built in a responsible manner.
explanation: The text reads like a marketing-style tech release with highly specific numbers (128K-token context, 15 trillion tokens) and bold claims such as parity with GPT-4. Plus invented terms like Llama Guard 3, which are typical hallmarks of AI-generated content.
